title: The Science of Multi-Dimensional Fitment: More Than Just a Mark Sheet
excerpt: Why do standard career tests fail? Discover how MyAarohan's 'Multi-Dimensional Fitment Model' integrates Aptitude, Interest, and Personality.
author: MyAarohan Team
content:
Imagine trying to build a house using only a 2D blueprint. It looks perfect on paper, but when you build, you realize the ground is swampy. The blueprint failed because it ignored reality. Career planning faces the same crisis.

Most guidance is Unidimensional (Marks only) or Bidimensional (Marks + Interest). At MyAarohan, we use a Multi-Dimensional Fitment Model. We believe true career satisfaction requires analyzing the invisible layers—Personality, Orientation, and Emotional Intelligence.

The Science of Scoring: Sten Scores

A raw score (e.g., 45/60) is meaningless without context. We convert scores to a Sten Score (Standard Ten Score), a universal translator for talent. It places a student on a scale of 1 to 10 compared to thousands of peers.

  • 1-3 (Low Range): Not a fail, but not a natural driver.
  • 4-7 (Average Range): The Growth Zone. Can be developed.
  • 8-10 (High Potential): The Flow Zone. Work feels effortless here.

The Evolution of Fitment

Our algorithm filters data through three layers:

  1. Dimension 1 (The Desire Filter): What do you love? (Interest)
  2. Dimension 2 (The Engine Filter): What can you naturally do? (Aptitude)
  3. Dimension 3 (The Nature Filter): Who are you at your core? (Personality)

The Twist

Consider a student who loves Law (Interest) and has the Logic for it (Aptitude). BUT, their Personality reveals low Resilience (sensitive to stress) and high Agreeableness (avoids conflict). A high-pressure Corporate Litigator role would cause burnout. Our recommendation? "Legal Research" or "Mediation"—roles that use the same aptitude but fit the personality.

Conclusion

Your child is not a statistic. They are a complex, multi-layered individual. We use the precision of Sten Scores and Multi-Dimensional Logic to ensure that when your child chooses a career, it fits every part of who they are.
